mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts revenue growth for S&P 500 constituents and generates positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 20-day equity returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which improves short-term earnings momentum and triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production revisions are followed by negative 5-day equity returns in > 55% of cases during early-cycle phases, or predictive R-squared remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 195.0 while the federal funds rate remains above 3.0%, the resulting real rate compression and margin squeeze on corporate profits forces equity valuation multiples down, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high inflation and elevated rates yields positive 60-day equity returns in > 60% of observations, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms expanding economic activity, which directly lifts corporate revenue streams and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 20-day equity returns in > 50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly nonfarm payrolls additions falling below 100,000 indicate labor market deceleration, which reduces consumer spending capacity and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Weak payroll growth fails to predict negative equity returns, with 20-day forward returns remaining positive in > 65% of triggered months, or out-of-sample hit rate drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month expansions in industrial production and retail sales signal synchronized supply-demand balance, which reduces inventory buildup risks and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive momentum in production and sales shows no statistically significant predictive power for 5-day equity returns, or the interaction term coefficient is negative in out-of-sample regressions.
